content: A ray of light is sent along the line $$x-2 y+5=0$$. Upon reaching the line $$3 x-2 y+7=0$$, the ray is reflected from it. The equation of the line containing the reflected ray, is
explanation:
The reflected by PB passes through $$(-1,2)$$

Let its slope be $$m$$.

Slope of $$P M=-\frac{2}{3}$$

[$$\because P M$$ is perpendicular to $$3 x-2 y+7=0$$]

$$\therefore \quad \tan \theta=\frac{\frac{1}{2}+\frac{2}{3}}{1-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{2}{3}}=\frac{7}{4}$$

$$\angle M P B=\theta$$ and this is the angle between $$P M$$ whose slope is $$-\frac{2}{3}$$ and $$P B$$ whose slope is $$m$$.

$$\begin{array}{ll} \therefore & \frac{-\frac{2}{3}-m}{1-\frac{2}{3} m}=\frac{7}{4} \\ \Rightarrow & 8+12 m=-21+14 m \\ \Rightarrow & m=\frac{29}{2} \end{array}$$

$$\therefore$$ Equation of $$P B$$, the reflected ray is

$$\begin{aligned} & y-2=\frac{29}{2}(x+1) \\ & \Rightarrow 2 y-4=29 x+29 \Rightarrow 29 x-2 y+33=0 \end{aligned}$$

content: The equation of a straight line which cuts off intercept on $$X$$-axis which is twice that on $$Y$$-axis and is at a unit distance from origin is given by
explanation:
Let the equation of straight line is

$$\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}=1 \quad \text{.... (i)}$$

where, $$a=$$ length of intercept on $$x$$-axis. and $$b=$$ length of intercept on $$y$$-axis. Now, by condition,

$$a=2 b \Rightarrow b=\frac{a}{2} \quad \text{.... (ii)}$$

Also, perpendicular distance from origin to line (i) $$=1$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{|0+0-1|}{\sqrt{\frac{1}{a^2}+\frac{1}{b^2}}}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{a^2}+\frac{1}{b^2}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{a^2}+\frac{4}{a^2}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a^2=5 \text {, } \\ & \text { [from Eq. (ii)]. } \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a= \pm \sqrt{5} \\ & \text { and } \\ & b= \pm \frac{\sqrt{5}}{2} \\ \end{aligned}$$

Now, from Eq. (i), we get

$$\frac{x}{ \pm \sqrt{5}} \pm \frac{y}{\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}\right)}=1$$

$$\begin{array}{lr} \Rightarrow & \frac{x}{( \pm \sqrt{5})}+\frac{2 y}{( \pm \sqrt{5})}=1 \\ \Rightarrow & x+2 y= \pm \sqrt{5} \\ \Rightarrow & x+2 y \pm \sqrt{5}=0 \end{array}$$

content: The equation of a straight line upon which the length of the perpendicular from the origin is 5 and slope of this perpendicular is 3/4 is
explanation:
Let $$y=m x+c$$ be the equation of straight line.

$$\Rightarrow \quad m x-y+c=0\quad \text{..... (i)}$$

Now, by condition

Perpendicular distance from origin to line (i) = 5

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{|0-0+c|}{\sqrt{m^2+1}}=5 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad c= \pm 5 \sqrt{m^2+1} \quad \text{.... (ii)} \end{aligned}$$

Also, given that

Slope of perpendicular to line (i) from the origin $$=\frac{3}{4}$$

$$\Rightarrow \quad-\frac{1}{m}=\frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow m=\frac{-4}{3}$$

From Eq. (ii),

$$c= \pm 5 \sqrt{1+\frac{16}{9}}= \pm 5 \times \frac{5}{3}= \pm \frac{25}{3}$$

On putting the values of $$m$$ and $$c$$ in Eq. (i), we get

$$\begin{aligned} & -\frac{4 x}{3}-y \pm \frac{25}{3}=0 \\ \Rightarrow \quad & 4 x+3 y \pm 25=0 \end{aligned}$$

which is required equation of straight line.
